---
title: "Core Concepts and Interpretation"
id: "binomial_distribution-se01"
toc: True
---

This session introduces the binomial distribution, its assumptions, interpretation,
and application in simple probability problems.


### Learning Objectives
<br>

- Explain what the binomial distribution represents


- Identify the conditions under which a binomial model is appropriate


- Interpret probabilities from a binomial distribution


- Use R to calculate binomial probabilities



<br>

<br>

<!-- START_NAVIGATION -->


```{=html}
<div class="custom-page-navigation">
    <div class="nav-prev">
        
        <a href="../index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">← Previous</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Binomial Distribution</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
    <div class="nav-next">
        
        <a href="01-core-concepts-and-interpretation/index.qmd">
            <span class="nav-label">Next →</span>
            <span class="nav-title">Core Concepts and Interpretation</span>
        </a>
        
    </div>
</div>
```

<!-- END_NAVIGATION -->
